title: Denmark Notification of Entry (CPR Registration) — Anmeldelse af Indrejse
description: Denmark's national form for notifying a municipality (kommune) of entry/moving to Denmark from abroad, form FR 050 ("Anmeldelse af indrejse"), published by KL (Kommunernes Landsforening, Local Government Denmark) and used uniformly across all Danish municipalities to register a person or family group in CPR (Det Centrale Personregister, the Central Person Register) on first moving to Denmark or returning after living abroad. Registering as entered ("indrejst") in CPR is what triggers assignment of a personnummer (CPR number) and issuance of a sundhedskort (health insurance card) for entrants who do not already have one. This schema models the form's own applicant-facing section in full: the shared entry-date/departure/destination-address block, and the repeating table of up to 6 entrants travelling together (name, gender, place of birth, citizenship, personnummer if already assigned, Nordic country-of-departure identification if applicable, and folkekirke/church-membership status for each). It deliberately does NOT model the form's own second section, printed under the heading "Andre oplysninger (udfyldes af kommunen)" ("Other information, completed by the municipality") — marital status, marriage documentation, spouse's personnummer, previous Danish address, date of departure from Denmark, police alien registration number, parents' personnummer, choice of physician, and the municipal caseworker's own notes/signature — because the form's own printed heading discloses these fields are filled in by municipal staff during in-person processing, not by the applicant. Submitting this notification requires in-person attendance at the bopælskommune (municipality of residence) with supporting identity and lawful-residence documentation per CPR-lovens §16-17 (§20-21); this schema does not submit the notification itself, and the live source (kl.dk / the applicant's own kommune) is always authoritative. GovSchema is independent and is not affiliated with, endorsed by, or operated by the Kingdom of Denmark, KL, or any Danish kommune.

operatedBy.name: Kommunernes Landsforening (KL) — Local Government Denmark, publisher of this form; the notification is submitted to, and CPR registration performed by, the applicant's own bopælskommune (municipality of residence)
operatedBy.basis: The form's own footer ("Udarbejdet af KL", KLE 23.05.12G01, FR 050 (12/2025)); CPR-administrationen's own published guidance (cpr.dk) confirms the bopælskommune determines eligibility for CPR entry-registration under CPR-lovens §16-17 and that personnummer assignment follows from that registration.

source.documentRef: Blanket FR 050 "Anmeldelse af indrejse" (December 2025 edition), a 2-page fillable AcroForm PDF published unauthenticated in KL's own blanket library (kl.dk/blanketter/blanketsamling/folkeregistrering), cross-checked against KL's own official English translation, FR 050_ENG "Notification of entry" (same edition, same host).

verification.notes: GOV-2260: GovSchema Standard Research cycle. Opens Denmark's National ID & Civic Documents vertical (3/6; Passport and Taxes already modelled via GOV-2244/GOV-2253). CATALOG.md's own Known Gaps item 4 had flagged Denmark's CPR system as an open, unscreened candidate, noting the personnummer already reused throughout the Passport and Taxes schemas. This cycle screened all three of Denmark's remaining open verticals in parallel (Business Formation via virk.dk form 40.110, a genuine unauthenticated AcroForm for foreign-business registration without MitID; Visa via SIRI's nyidanmark.dk work-permit form AR8, genuine and unauthenticated but a fill-by-hand PDF with no AcroForm widget layer; National ID via KL's FR 050 CPR/Folkeregister entry-notification form) and picked FR 050 as the strongest: a single national AcroForm template used uniformly across all ~98 Danish kommuner (not a local variant), with a genuine unauthenticated official English cross-check translation (FR 050_ENG) and direct composability with the personnummer field type already established by this registry's DK schemas. Fetched fresh (both fr050.pdf, 85,513 bytes, and fr050_eng.pdf, 88,602 bytes, HTTP 200, no login/CAPTCHA/WAF gate) and extracted with pdfjs-dist getAnnotations()/getTextContent() per page rather than trusting the scouting pass's own field count: page 1 carries 80 real AcroForm widgets (78 Tx text fields + a link annotation + one further Tx field split across the page-1/page-2 boundary), page 2 carries 2 (a Datatilsynet.dk link annotation and one free-text "kommune noteringer" field). Cross-referencing every widget's alternativeText/fieldName against the page's own printed labels (via getTextContent(), matched by y-coordinate) established a clean, disclosed structural finding: the form's own page-1 heading "Andre oplysninger (udfyldes af kommunen)" ("Other information, completed by the municipality") explicitly marks every field from Ægteskabelig status (marital status) onward — marriage documentation, spouse's personnummer, previous Danish address, date of departure, police alien-registration number, both parents' personnummer, choice of physician, caseworker remarks/contact/signature — as municipal-staff-entered, not applicant-entered; each such field's own alternativeText independently confirms this (e.g. "Ægteskabelig status på de indrejsende udfyldes af kommunen"). This schema models only the applicant-facing section above that heading: the shared entry-date/departure/destination-address block (12 fields) and the repeating table of up to 6 entrants travelling together (8 fields each: personnummer, surname, given names, gender, place of birth, citizenship, folkekirke membership, and Nordic-country-of-departure identification — 48 fields), for 60 fields total, matching the table's own printed "1 2 3 4 5 6" column numbering exactly (verified against both the Danish and English specimens). Two disclosed modelling notes: (1) personnummer is left optional for every entrant, not just deferred ones, because CPR-administrationen's own published guidance (cpr.dk, fetched directly this cycle) states a personnummer is assigned upon successful CPR entry-registration — i.e. this notification is frequently how a first-time entrant obtains one, so the field cannot be assumed pre-filled; (2) "Medlem af folkekirken" is modelled as a single boolean per entrant even though the source PDF implements it as two independent checkbox widgets (a Ja box and a Nej box, not a true radio group) that happen to share the same AcroForm export value "ja" — a specimen quirk disclosed here rather than silently corrected. Money/date types and validation conventions (personnummer pattern ^[0-9]{10}$, postal-code pattern ^[0-9]{4}$) follow this registry's existing dk/skattestyrelsen and dk/um precedent. A mock conformance run (see this document's own VERIFICATION.md) filled all 60 fields with valid synthetic example values for a family of two entering Denmark from Sweden and validated cleanly against the schema and against tools/validate.mjs.

fields.name: entryDate
fields.label: Indrejsedato
fields.description: The date of entry into Denmark (day/month/year) for the group of persons covered by this notification.
fields.sourceRef: Header — Indrejsedato (Dag / måned / år)

fields.name: countryOfDeparture
fields.label: Fraflytningsland
fields.description: The country the group is moving from.
fields.sourceRef: "Flytter fra" section — Fraflytningsland

fields.name: departureAddress
fields.label: Fraflytningsadresse
fields.description: The group's address in the country of departure.
fields.sourceRef: "Flytter fra" section — Fraflytningsadresse

fields.name: movingToStreetName
fields.label: Vejnavn
fields.description: The street name of the Danish address the group is moving to.
fields.sourceRef: "Flytter til" section — Vejnavn

fields.name: movingToHouseNumber
fields.label: Husnr./-bogstav
fields.description: The house number (and letter, if any) of the Danish address the group is moving to.
fields.sourceRef: "Flytter til" section — Husnr./-bogstav

fields.name: movingToFloor
fields.label: Etage
fields.description: The floor of the Danish address the group is moving to, if the building has floors.
fields.sourceRef: "Flytter til" section — Etage

fields.name: movingToSideDoorNumber
fields.label: Side/dørnummer
fields.description: The side and/or door number of the Danish address the group is moving to, if applicable.
fields.sourceRef: "Flytter til" section — Side/dørnummer

fields.name: movingToPostalCode
fields.label: Postnummer
fields.description: The postal code of the Danish address the group is moving to.
fields.sourceRef: "Flytter til" section — Postnummer

fields.name: movingToPostalDistrict
fields.label: Postdistrikt
fields.description: The postal district (by name) of the Danish address the group is moving to.
fields.sourceRef: "Flytter til" section — Postdistrikt

fields.name: movingToCityName
fields.label: Evt. bynavn
fields.description: The city/town name of the Danish address the group is moving to, if applicable (some rural Danish addresses are identified by postal district alone).
fields.sourceRef: "Flytter til" section — Evt. bynavn

fields.name: movingToCareOfAddress
fields.label: Bor hos (c/o)
fields.description: The name and address of the person the group will be living with (c/o), if the new residence is not solely the group's own.
fields.sourceRef: "Flytter til" section — Bor hos (c/o)

fields.name: expectedDurationOfStay
fields.label: Opholdets forventede varighed
fields.description: The group's expected duration of stay in Denmark. Per CPR-lovens §16, registration in CPR on entry is only possible if the stay is expected to exceed 3 months (or 6 months for Nordic/EU/EEA/Swiss citizens), so this value is central to whether the notification itself is applicable.
fields.sourceRef: "Flytter til" section — Opholdets forventede varighed

fields.name: entrant1CivilRegistrationNumber
fields.label: Personnummer
fields.description: The first entrant's Danish CPR number (personnummer), if one has already been assigned (e.g. by Udlændingestyrelsen/SIRI as part of a residence-permit case, or from a prior period of Danish residence). Left optional: per CPR-administrationen's own guidance (cpr.dk), a personnummer is often assigned only once this notification is processed and the applicant is registered as "indrejst" in CPR, so a genuinely first-time entrant may have none to report yet.
fields.sourceRef: Table column "Personnummer", første person / entrant 1 of up to 6

fields.name: entrant1Surname
fields.label: Efternavn
fields.description: The first entrant's surname/last name.
fields.sourceRef: Table column "Efternavn", første person / entrant 1 of up to 6

fields.name: entrant1GivenNames
fields.label: Samtlige fornavne og mellemnavne
fields.description: All of the first entrant's first names and middle names.
fields.sourceRef: Table column "Samtlige fornavne og mellemnavne", første person / entrant 1 of up to 6

fields.name: entrant1Gender
fields.label: Køn
fields.description: The first entrant's gender, as recorded on this form.
fields.sourceRef: Table column "Køn", første person / entrant 1 of up to 6

fields.name: entrant1PlaceOfBirth
fields.label: Fødested
fields.description: The first entrant's place of birth.
fields.sourceRef: Table column "Fødested", første person / entrant 1 of up to 6

fields.name: entrant1Citizenship
fields.label: Statsborgerforhold
fields.description: The first entrant's citizenship(s).
fields.sourceRef: Table column "Statsborgerforhold", første person / entrant 1 of up to 6

fields.name: entrant1MemberOfFolkekirken
fields.label: Medlem af folkekirken
fields.description: Whether the first entrant is a member of the Evangelical-Lutheran Church in Denmark (Folkekirken). On the source PDF this is two separate checkbox widgets (a "Ja" box and a "Nej" box) rather than one shared toggle or true radio group; both widgets happen to share the AcroForm export value "ja" (a specimen quirk, disclosed rather than corrected), and this schema models the pair as the single boolean answer they jointly represent.
fields.sourceRef: "Medlem af folkekirken" Ja/Nej checkbox pair, første person / entrant 1 of up to 6

fields.name: entrant1CivilRegistrationNumberInCountryOfDeparture
fields.label: Identifikation (personnummer) i fraflytningslandet
fields.description: If the first entrant is moving to Denmark from a Nordic country, their civil registration/identification number in that country of departure.
fields.sourceRef: "Hvis du tilflytter fra et nordisk land, bedes du anføre din identifikation (personnummer) i fraflytningslandet" column, første person / entrant 1 of up to 6
